hyperedges.label: Gated CI pipeline stages (quality+tests block the build matrix)
Self-hosted DNS stack (dnsdist :53 / pdns internal / caddy :80-:443)

nodes.label: Agent Orchestration Architecture (Engineering Manager dispatcher)
nodes.rationale: All user requests must be delegated to the engineering-manager agent which orchestrates backend-dev, frontend-dev, code-reviewer and expert agents; keeps a single interlocutor and consistent French communication.

nodes.label: Audit Mission: WebRTC DataChannel HEVC Pipeline (Android latency + pixelation)
nodes.rationale: Drop is accepted over accumulation; artifacts < 300ms accepted; no regression on PC/Mac/iOS; audit first, no code changes.
nodes.norm_label: audit mission: webrtc datachannel hevc pipeline (android latency + pixelation)

nodes.label: Problem A: Latency Accumulation (buffer bloat on Android)
nodes.rationale: Three unbounded/poorly bounded buffers stack invisibly: Qt worker-to-main event queue, SCTP buffer inflated by maxRetransmits=3, and the VideoDecoder internal queue (decodeQueueSize never consulted) which is the dominant mechanism.
nodes.norm_label: problem a: latency accumulation (buffer bloat on android)

nodes.label: Problem B: Persistent Blocky Pixelation (lost reference frames)
nodes.rationale: Neither end drops deltas after a reference loss: backend drop is non-sticky, frontend drops incomplete frames silently without signaling; Android HW decoder decodes orphan P-frames without emitting errors so the existing recovery never triggers.
nodes.norm_label: problem b: persistent blocky pixelation (lost reference frames)

nodes.label: Audit Deliverable 3: Prioritized Optimization Plan (OPT-0 to OPT-20)
nodes.rationale: Global invariant from code review: a keyframe is NEVER dropped, by any mechanism, at any stage.
nodes.norm_label: audit deliverable 3: prioritized optimization plan (opt-0 to opt-20)

nodes.label: OPT-0b: Backend IDR Request Coalescing (cooldown 250-500ms + relaunch)
nodes.rationale: After P0 fixes up to 6 sources will request IDRs; without a single convergence point in DataChannelRelay::requestIdrFrame() with temporal cooldown, LiRequestIdrFrame floods Sunshine (regression already experienced: reorder buffer IDR flood).
nodes.norm_label: opt-0b: backend idr request coalescing (cooldown 250-500ms + relaunch)

nodes.label: OPT-1: maxRetransmits=0 on Video DataChannel (both sides)
nodes.rationale: Under loss SCTP retransmits stale data up to 3 times, inflating bufferedAmount and creating de facto head-of-line blocking; no reference implementation retransmits realtime video. Negotiated channel id=0 requires both sides to match.
nodes.norm_label: opt-1: maxretransmits=0 on video datachannel (both sides)

nodes.label: OPT-3: Sticky m_AwaitingIdr Flag (backend)
nodes.rationale: Current delta drop is not sticky: the next delta is sent as soon as bufferedAmount falls although its reference was never transmitted, producing orphan P-frames and persistent macro-blocks. Modeled on moonlight-common-c waitingForIdrFrame.

nodes.label: OPT-4: _referenceValid State + frameId Gap Detection (frontend)
nodes.rationale: Lever #1 frontend for Problem B: Android HW decoder displays orphan deltas without emitting errors so _handleDecoderError never triggers. Deliberately redundant with OPT-3 (defense in depth, backend cannot cover pure SCTP losses post-backend).

nodes.label: OPT-5: Bound the Qt Worker-to-Main Signal Queue (m_PendingFrames)
nodes.rationale: Qt event queue is unbounded and invisible to the SCTP watermark: main thread blocked 100ms means ~6 frames piled up then sent in burst. Bound 2-3 with atomic counter; keyframes never dropped here (SPS/PPS loss = null decoder, known regression).
nodes.norm_label: opt-5: bound the qt worker-to-main signal queue (m_pendingframes)

nodes.label: Pattern: Drop Deltas Before Decoder Until IDR (waitingForIdrFrame / needsKeyFrame)
nodes.rationale: All three reference implementations (moonlight-qt waitingForIdrFrame, moonlight-xbox short queue + priority IDR, moonlight-web-stream needsKeyFrame) drop delta frames BEFORE the decoder while recovery is pending. This is the central missing mechanism in Moonlight-Web.
nodes.norm_label: pattern: drop deltas before decoder until idr (waitingforidrframe / needskeyframe)

nodes.label: Audit Deliverable 4: Sequenced Implementation Roadmap (6 steps)
nodes.rationale: Step 2 (recovery) must precede step 3 (latency) because step 3 drop mechanisms arm the awaiting-IDR states created in step 2; reverse order would produce drops without recovery. Risk trio validated at each step: Chrome Windows HEVC, Safari iOS, lossy mobile network.
nodes.norm_label: audit deliverable 4: sequenced implementation roadmap (6 steps)

nodes.label: deSEC Dynamic DNS Registration (moonlightweb-{uniqueId}.dedyn.io)
nodes.rationale: Persistent per-machine public domain built from the existing uniqueId; A record updated every 5 minutes when STUN-detected public IP changes.

nodes.label: Public IP Detection via STUN Fallback Chain
nodes.norm_label: public ip detection via stun fallback chain

nodes.label: UPnP Port Mapping with CGNAT Detection
nodes.rationale: UPnP failure must not block startup; double NAT detected by comparing UPnP external IP with STUN public IP.
nodes.norm_label: upnp port mapping with cgnat detection

nodes.label: Transport Protocol Auto-Selection Priority (MediaTrack UDP > DC UDP > MediaTrack TCP > DC TCP > WSS)
nodes.rationale: No TURN candidates ever - direct connection only; UDP candidates forced before TCP in SDP; codec validated against RTCRtpReceiver.getCapabilities before committing to a transport.
nodes.norm_label: transport protocol auto-selection priority (mediatrack udp > dc udp > mediatrack tcp > dc tcp > wss)

nodes.label: GDPR Consent Step for Internet Access
nodes.rationale: Sharing the public IP with deSEC and enabling UPnP require explicit opt-in (default OFF); refusing consent disables all external calls.
nodes.norm_label: gdpr consent step for internet access

nodes.label: Let's Encrypt TLS via DNS-01 Challenge (deSEC)
nodes.rationale: DNS-01 avoids needing port 80 open; auto-renewal when < 30 days remain, TLS reloaded without restart.
nodes.norm_label: let's encrypt tls via dns-01 challenge (desec)

nodes.label: Limelight C Library (moonlight-common-c protocol core)
nodes.rationale: Handles the full GameStream protocol (RTSP handshake, RTP video/audio reassembly+FEC, ENet control); Moonlight-Web embeds it directly instead of reimplementing RTSP in JS.

nodes.label: Two-Renderer Pipeline Pattern (backend decode + frontend render)
nodes.rationale: Backend renderer does accelerated decoding via AVCodecContext hw_frames_ctx; frontend renderer displays decoded AVFrames (CSC, scaling, HDR); they can be the same object (CUDA) or separate (VAAPI + Vulkan).
nodes.norm_label: two-renderer pipeline pattern (backend decode + frontend render)

nodes.label: Decision: Video Passthrough (no backend decode/re-encode)
nodes.rationale: Forward Annex B NAL units assembled by drSubmitDecodeUnit directly to the browser; backend re-encoding would add 30ms+ latency and huge complexity.

nodes.label: Decision: Simple Binary WebSocket Protocol [channel:1][flags:1][payload:N]
nodes.rationale: moonlight-common-c uses proprietary RTP+custom FEC; adapting to WebRTC would require modifying it. A single multiplexed WS connection suffices on LAN where head-of-line blocking is negligible. (Later superseded by WebRTC DataChannels for Internet mode.)

nodes.label: Decision: Forward PCM Audio instead of Opus
nodes.rationale: moonlight-common-c already decodes Opus multistream to PCM in arDecodeAndPlaySample; forwarding PCM avoids shipping a ~1MB Opus WASM decoder and reimplementing multistream demuxing in JS.
nodes.norm_label: decision: forward pcm audio instead of opus

nodes.label: Decision: Native ENet Control Channel for Input
nodes.rationale: Browser input arrives as JSON over WS, is encoded by InputEncoder, encrypted AES-128-GCM by InputCrypto, then sent to Sunshine via the ENet embedded in moonlight-common-c (START_A/B handshake) instead of a WebSocket bridge.
nodes.norm_label: decision: native enet control channel for input

nodes.label: zrok Bootstrap-Only Architecture (discovery + signaling, never the stream)
nodes.rationale: zrok exposes only the HTTPS UI and ICE/STUN signaling bootstrap with a persistent URL moonlightweb-{UniqueID}.share.zrok.io; video/audio/input traffic must never transit zrok.
nodes.norm_label: zrok bootstrap-only architecture (discovery + signaling, never the stream)

nodes.label: ENET UDP P2P Streaming after Hole Punching (zrok plan)
nodes.rationale: Target was STUN + UDP hole punching then direct ENet P2P without a WebRTC video pipeline; later architecture decisions replaced this with WebRTC DataChannels since browser P2P UDP is impossible without WebRTC.
nodes.norm_label: enet udp p2p streaming after hole punching (zrok plan)

nodes.label: 4-Phase PIN Pairing Protocol (AES-ECB + RSA/SHA-256)
nodes.rationale: Phases run over HTTP (47989) not HTTPS; phase 1 blocks server-side until the user enters the PIN; AES key = SHA256(salt+PIN)[:16]; real client identity is the X.509 certificate, uniqueid is a shared constant 0123456789ABCDEF.

nodes.label: /launch Endpoint (rikey AES-GCM session key, returns RTSP sessionUrl0)
nodes.norm_label: /launch endpoint (rikey aes-gcm session key, returns rtsp sessionurl0)

nodes.label: Video Path Document: WebRTC DataChannel over ICE-TCP
nodes.norm_label: video path document: webrtc datachannel over ice-tcp

nodes.label: ICE-TCP Fallback Transport (RFC 6544, active backend / passive browser)
nodes.rationale: Enabled only in Internet mode (enableIceTcp=true); disabled on LAN because it caused PeerConnection failures with some libdatachannel versions. TCP candidates have lower ICE priority and are selected only when all UDP pairs fail.
nodes.norm_label: ice-tcp fallback transport (rfc 6544, active backend / passive browser)

nodes.label: Video Chunk Fragmentation Header (17 bytes: frame_id, chunk, total, is_keyframe, payload, backend_ts)
nodes.norm_label: video chunk fragmentation header (17 bytes: frame_id, chunk, total, is_keyframe, payload, backend_ts)

nodes.label: Frontend App Icon / PWA Icon Usage
nodes.norm_label: frontend app icon / pwa icon usage

nodes.label: Planet-with-Orbital-Ring Motif (Saturn-like sphere with 8-spoke wheel pattern, flat slate-gray monochrome style)
nodes.norm_label: planet-with-orbital-ring motif (saturn-like sphere with 8-spoke wheel pattern, flat slate-gray monochrome style)

nodes.label: CI Pipeline (gated quality → tests → build)
nodes.rationale: Single gated pipeline so the expensive multi-platform build matrix is only spent on validated code; PR runs are cancel-superseded but pushes on main are never cancelled because they feed releases.

nodes.label: CI Frontend job (Prettier + ESLint + Vitest, 70% gate)
nodes.rationale: Frontend quality and tests share one job: tests gate on quality anyway and a single npm install/Node setup serves both.
nodes.norm_label: ci frontend job (prettier + eslint + vitest, 70% gate)

nodes.label: CI C++ quality jobs (clang-format 19.1.7 + cppcheck)
nodes.rationale: clang-format pinned to the same major.minor as the LLVM bundled with VS2022 to avoid formatting disagreements across versions.

nodes.label: CI Backend tests job (Qt Test + OpenCppCoverage, 70% gate)
nodes.norm_label: ci backend tests job (qt test + opencppcoverage, 70% gate)

nodes.label: CI multi-platform build (Windows x64/ARM64, Linux x64, macOS arm64)
nodes.rationale: Ninja + msvc-dev-cmd used instead of the version-pinned 'Visual Studio NN' generator because the runner's VS major version changes over time; macos-15 pinned (not macos-latest) so the June 2026 macOS 26 migration cannot silently change the runner.
nodes.norm_label: ci multi-platform build (windows x64/arm64, linux x64, macos arm64)

nodes.label: aqtinstall commit pin + py7zr 1.1.0 workaround
nodes.rationale: aqt 3.3.0 doesn't know the new Qt 6.11 per-arch repo layout (404s on Updates.xml) so the commit that added it is pinned; py7zr 1.1.0 avoids the 7z path-sanitization bug (aqtinstall#995) that breaks SBOM extraction; --base https://download.qt.io because fallback mirrors serve incomplete XML.

nodes.label: MW_* build-time secrets embedding (DNS/ACME config)
nodes.source_location: Configure step env (MW_DOMAIN, MW_PDNS_URL, MW_PDNS_TOKEN, MW_ZEROSSL_EAB_*)
nodes.rationale: Internet-Access config is embedded at build time from repo secrets (no .env shipped); an unset secret yields an empty value which falls back to LAN-only mode. Secrets are compiled into MoonlightWeb.exe, never passed to installers, so nothing on the user's machine carries them.

nodes.label: Release packaging workflow (v* tags → installers)
nodes.rationale: On a v* tag builds self-contained bundles (Windows installer, Linux deb/rpm/AppImage, macOS dmg) and attaches them to the GitHub Release; workflow_dispatch platform choice lets a single platform be rebuilt; version derived from the tag (v1.2.3 → 1.2.3).

nodes.label: Windows Inno Setup installer (x64 + ARM64 matrix)
nodes.rationale: Interactive stepped wizard (Internet authorization, Sunshine detection/silent-install + pairing, admin shortcut); it is the only Windows artifact shipped — no raw zip.
nodes.norm_label: windows inno setup installer (x64 + arm64 matrix)

nodes.label: Linux packaging (.deb + .rpm + AppImage from one AppDir)
nodes.rationale: One linuxdeploy AppDir (bundled Qt, $ORIGIN rpaths) feeds all three outputs: deb for Debian/Ubuntu/Mint, rpm for Fedora/RHEL, AppImage as portable fallback for Arch and immutable gaming distros. cmake --install applies frontend excludes (node_modules, tests, .claude) — never copy frontend/ straight from checkout. Qt Wayland plugins bundled best-effort so native Wayland sessions don't fall back to XWayland.
nodes.norm_label: linux packaging (.deb + .rpm + appimage from one appdir)

nodes.label: macOS .app bundle + ad-hoc inside-out codesign + DMG
nodes.rationale: Frontend assets go in Contents/Resources (codesign treats everything under Contents/MacOS as code). codesign --deep must NOT be used: Qt binaries from aqt are signed with The Qt Company's Team ID while the main binary became ad-hoc, so dyld rejected them ('mapping process and mapped file have different Team IDs') and the app crashed — instead each Mach-O is force re-signed ad-hoc inside-out, bundle last. DMG built with hdiutil because a second macdeployqt -dmg would re-deploy and invalidate the signature.
nodes.norm_label: macos .app bundle + ad-hoc inside-out codesign + dmg

nodes.label: Force-deploy Qt OpenSSL TLS plugin (qopensslbackend.dll)
nodes.rationale: windeployqt ships only the schannel/certonly TLS backends; the OpenSSL backend plugin is force-copied so QSslSocket::setActiveBackend("openssl") in main.cpp succeeds — otherwise Qt falls back to Schannel, which cannot load the public ACME PEM key, and the browser is served the self-signed cert (CN/SAN error).

nodes.label: Contributing guide (toolchain, build, tests, PR workflow)
nodes.norm_label: contributing guide (toolchain, build, tests, pr workflow)

nodes.label: CMake as single canonical build system
nodes.rationale: Cross-platform build via CMake, qmake removed; CMake also generates compile_commands.json for clangd/IDEs. Requires CMake >= 3.21, Ninja, Qt 6.11 with the WebSockets module, Node.js 22 for frontend tooling.
nodes.norm_label: cmake as single canonical build system

nodes.label: MoonlightWeb — 100% web Sunshine/GameStream client
nodes.author: Bruno Martin
nodes.rationale: Turns any device with a modern browser into a streaming client for a Sunshine gaming PC with nothing to install; GPL-3.0; author previously upstreamed Video Super Resolution across all major Moonlight clients.

nodes.label: MoonlightWeb server (C++/Qt backend)
nodes.rationale: The server is three things at once: a web server (frontend + REST API), a proxy to Sunshine's API, and a streaming bridge embedding moonlight-common-c. Runs on any machine on the same LAN as Sunshine; installing on the Sunshine machine is ideal (localhost latency, instant mDNS) but not required.

nodes.label: moonlight-common-c (embedded GameStream protocol library)
nodes.rationale: Embedded in the backend to speak GameStream (RTSP/RTP/ENet) to Sunshine; provides Limelight.h as a git submodule.

nodes.label: WebRTC transport (DataChannels + RTP media tracks)
nodes.rationale: Video (H.264/HEVC/AV1) and Opus audio relayed over WebRTC; video decodes in WebCodecs + WebGPU/canvas, audio in AudioWorklet; input encrypted AES-128-GCM to Sunshine over the ENet control channel.

nodes.label: Internet Access feature (auto sub-domain + TLS + UPnP)
nodes.rationale: One-click: detect public IP (STUN, HTTPS fallback), create subdomain {id}.yourdomain via PowerDNS API (A record + TXT ownership token), obtain TLS cert via ACME DNS-01, open ports via UPnP (TCP 80/443 + UDP 47999). Known limits: UPnP disabled, CGNAT/double-NAT (detected and reported), restrictive corporate networks.
nodes.norm_label: internet access feature (auto sub-domain + tls + upnp)

nodes.label: Video Enhancement (WebGPU upscaling FSR1/SGSRv1 + sharpening)
nodes.rationale: Browser-side GPU image enhancement to regain sharpness when stream resolution differs from display resolution.

nodes.label: Admin page (localhost-only server configuration)
nodes.rationale: Reachable only from the local machine; all /api/admin/* routes return 403 for non-localhost requests. Controls admin PIN, sessions, ports, transport, Internet access, certificate token.

nodes.label: settings.json (server-side settings store)
nodes.rationale: All settings stored server-side; hidden keys: domain (custom FQDN), cert_pem/cert_key (own certificate — CN must match domain, not auto-renewed), audio_time_stretch, http_port/https_port, stun_server.

nodes.label: Self-hosted DNS stack (dnsdist + PowerDNS + Caddy, Docker)
nodes.rationale: Three dedicated containers, one process each (idiomatic Docker). The MoonlightWeb server is NOT in this stack — it talks to the DNS box over the REST API (MW_PDNS_URL + MW_PDNS_TOKEN) and creates per-instance subdomains {uniqueId}.{MW_DOMAIN} at runtime; the stack only bootstraps the parent zone, NS glue, api host and www record. Hardening: dnsdist drops clients over ~50 qps and forces ANY→TCP; Caddy throttles the API to 60 req/min/IP; pdns runs non-root with cap_drop ALL; volumetric DDoS and host-level fail2ban are explicitly out of scope.
nodes.norm_label: self-hosted dns stack (dnsdist + powerdns + caddy, docker)

nodes.label: pdns container (PowerDNS authoritative + REST API, internal-only)
nodes.rationale: Official powerdns/pdns-auth-49 image unmodified; DNS on internal 5300, REST API on 8081, never published to the host. Fixed IP 172.28.0.10 because dnsdist's newServer needs a literal address (it does not resolve container names). MW_PDNS_API_KEY mapped to PDNS_AUTH_API_KEY so the key reaches the official mechanism unchanged; init.sh zone bootstrap is idempotent.
nodes.norm_label: pdns container (powerdns authoritative + rest api, internal-only)

nodes.label: dnsdist container (public :53 front, anti-amplification)
nodes.rationale: Public DNS entry point with per-client rate limiting and ANY→TCP to neutralise reflection/amplification; FD limit raised to 16384 because dnsdist pre-allocates ~10k sockets for per-IP QPS tracking.

nodes.label: caddy container (HTTPS API proxy + presentation website)
nodes.rationale: Caddy + caddy-ratelimit (xcaddy build) exposes the PowerDNS API as https://api.{MW_DOMAIN} with automatic Let's Encrypt TLS and request rate limiting, and serves the static presentation website at the apex/www (repo-root website/ bind-mounted, editable without rebuild). User-supplied cert files (MW_TLS_CERT/KEY, both set) take priority over automatic issuance.
nodes.norm_label: caddy container (https api proxy + presentation website)

nodes.label: MoonlightWeb presentation website (static landing page)
nodes.rationale: Plain HTML/CSS marketing page (Qt green / cyber cyan theme, OG tags, screenshots) served by the Caddy container at the apex domain; www permanently redirects to apex.

nodes.label: UPnP NAT Traversal test plan (Phase 7)
nodes.rationale: Covers unit fallback without miniupnpc, E2E with a real IGD, REST settings (upnp_enabled), /start response fields (upnpAvailable/upnpPublicIP/upnpPort), LAN regression (no candidate rewrite on LAN), external 4G/5G streaming, and robustness (port fallback 48010→48014, 30-min renewal timer, CGNAT detection, cleanup on shutdown).

nodes.label: UPNPClient (backend UPnP wrapper: discover/addPortMapping/getExternalIPAddress)
nodes.rationale: Must degrade gracefully when built without miniupnpc (isAvailable()=false, all ops return false/empty, no crash); host ICE candidates are rewritten LAN_IP → PUBLIC_IP:48010 by DataChannelRelay only when UPnP mapping succeeded.

nodes.label: UI audit 2026-06-17 (look & feel, file architecture, i18n)
nodes.rationale: Audit with hard invariants: same navigation, same wording, same behavior, same visual information — only look & feel, file architecture and i18n are open. Found 15 button variants, duplicated modules (js/api vs js/utils BackendClient, two StreamViews), monolithic 1500-line style.css, native prompt()/confirm() breaking the design.
nodes.norm_label: ui audit 2026-06-17 (look & feel, file architecture, i18n)

nodes.label: Design-system refonte (tokens + component grammar + split CSS)
nodes.rationale: Dark slate neutral + single accent, elevation by luminosity, glassmorphism generalized; one .btn/.card/.field/.badge/.dialog grammar replacing 15 button variants; target CSS tree tokens → base → layout → components → views/* → stream loaded in that order by index.html; emojis replaced by an inline SVG icon set; focus-visible and prefers-reduced-motion added.
nodes.norm_label: design-system refonte (tokens + component grammar + split css)

nodes.label: i18n stack decision: i18next engine + Tolgee self-hosted editor
nodes.rationale: ~200 hardcoded English strings (plus stray French toasts in app.js) to externalize as t('key') calls without changing wording; decided i18next as engine and Tolgee self-hosted (Docker) as web editor with build-time JSON pull; the shipped implementation ultimately replaced i18next with a homemade ~90-line runtime while keeping the Tolgee-compatible JSON_I18NEXT format.
nodes.norm_label: i18n stack decision: i18next engine + tolgee self-hosted editor

nodes.label: WebRTC HEVC audit — session summary (Engineering Manager)
nodes.rationale: Orchestration lessons: expert agents on broad missions exhaust their budget before reporting — bound missions to <=5 questions with files named and 'write your result before the final message'; subagents lacked write permission outside docs/ so results were archived in the audit folder. Code-reviewer corrected the audit itself: the commented P-frame zero-fill does not exist in the code.
nodes.norm_label: webrtc hevc audit — session summary (engineering manager)

nodes.label: WebRTC HEVC audit — deliverable 1: pipeline cartography
nodes.rationale: Frame-by-frame map Sunshine → MoonlightShim (worker thread, unbounded Qt signal queue) → DataChannelRelay (17-byte header, 64000-byte chunks, 128KB watermark, non-sticky delta drop) → SCTP (unordered, maxRetransmits=3 on both sides contradicting their own comments) → WebRtcDataChannel.js (silent drop of incomplete frames) → StreamView.js (decodeQueueSize never consulted, synthetic 60fps timestamps) → Canvas 2D (18 platform HEVC workarounds); gap table vs moonlight-qt/xbox/web-stream references.
nodes.norm_label: webrtc hevc audit — deliverable 1: pipeline cartography

nodes.label: WebRTC HEVC audit — deliverable 2: findings report (F1-F20, F1.x-F4.x)
nodes.rationale: Root cause A: three unbounded/mis-bounded buffers in cascade (Qt event queue, SCTP buffer inflated by maxRetransmits=3, VideoDecoder internal queue — the dominant one). Root cause B: neither end drops deltas after reference loss (non-sticky backend drop + silent frontend drop), and the Android HW decoder decodes orphan deltas without emitting any error so the error-based recovery never fires.
nodes.norm_label: webrtc hevc audit — deliverable 2: findings report (f1-f20, f1.x-f4.x)

nodes.label: WebRTC HEVC audit — deliverable 3: prioritized optimization plan (OPT-0..20)
nodes.rationale: Rules: drop accepted over accumulation; artifact < 300 ms accepted; NO regression PC/Mac/iOS; no over-engineering. Global invariant added by the reviewer: a keyframe is NEVER dropped, by any mechanism, at any stage. OPT-0a fixes lying code comments first because the audit itself was misled by them.
nodes.norm_label: webrtc hevc audit — deliverable 3: prioritized optimization plan (opt-0..20)

nodes.label: OPT-2 — Decoder backpressure: consult decodeQueueSize before decode()
nodes.rationale: Lever #1 of problem A: the VideoDecoder's internal queue is the dominant unbounded buffer; the existing frameQueue cap of 3 acts after decoding — right pattern, wrong stage. Threshold 3-4 (reviewer: 2-3 too aggressive for iOS HW decoder pipeline latency); reset criterion from web-stream: decodeQueueSize × (1000/fps) > 200 ms → reset + reconfigure + mandatory IDR; keyframes never dropped on this criterion.
nodes.norm_label: opt-2 — decoder backpressure: consult decodequeuesize before decode()

nodes.label: OPT-4 — Frontend referenceValid state + frameId gap detection
nodes.rationale: Lever #1 of problem B frontend-side: the Android HW decoder displays orphan deltas without emitting an error, so _handleDecoderError never fires. WebRtcDataChannel signals every definitive frame drop (onFrameLoss) and requests an IDR immediately instead of waiting for the 200/500 ms timers; StreamView drops deltas before decode() while _referenceValid is false, restored only by a keyframe. Deliberately redundant with OPT-3 (defense in depth — the backend cannot cover pure post-backend SCTP losses).
nodes.norm_label: opt-4 — frontend referencevalid state + frameid gap detection

nodes.label: Problem A — Android latency accumulation (buffer bloat)
nodes.rationale: Display lags behind received frames as if the pipeline forces itself to process every image within a growing buffer; expected behavior is to drop frames rather than accumulate latency.
nodes.norm_label: problem a — android latency accumulation (buffer bloat)

nodes.label: Latency audit — webrtc-media transport (RTP track + native <video>)
nodes.rationale: The webrtc-media path is already near-optimal browser-side (native HW decode, jitter buffer disabled); remaining margins are RTP pacing/timestamps, a misapplied render lever, a thread hop + copies, and A/V sync of audio-over-SCTP. Out of scope: network RTT, Sunshine encode latency, absence of REMB/TWCC congestion loop (bitrate stays governed by Sunshine via RTSP config).
nodes.norm_label: latency audit — webrtc-media transport (rtp track + native <video>)

nodes.label: MediaTrackRelay (backend RTP H.264 track relay)
nodes.rationale: Sends frames via H264RtpPacketizer → RtcpNackResponder(128) → PliHandler → SRTP; buffers the first keyframe until the track opens; m_RtpTimestamp += 1500 is a synthetic 60fps counter.

nodes.label: WebRtcMedia.js (frontend media-track client, <video> sink)
nodes.rationale: Sets jitterBufferTarget=0 on receivers and attaches the track to a native <video>; audio was PCM16 over a reliable ordered DataChannel at audit time (HoL blocking + A/V desync risks, Opus RTP track recommended as target).

nodes.label: P1-A — playoutDelayHint set on the wrong object (video element vs RTCRtpReceiver)
nodes.rationale: playoutDelayHint does not exist on HTMLVideoElement — it is an RTCRtpReceiver property (Chrome); the assignment was a silent no-op so Chrome kept its default playout delay. Setting it to 0 on the receiver engages the low-delay path and can remove tens of ms end-to-end; highest gain, zero risk (~3 lines).
nodes.norm_label: p1-a — playoutdelayhint set on the wrong object (video element vs rtcrtpreceiver)

nodes.label: P1-B — RTP timestamp frozen at synthetic 60 fps
nodes.rationale: m_RtpTimestamp += 1500 (90000/60) ignores the configurable stream FPS (30/120) and the real arrival jitter, so the browser's media clock/pacing and A/V sync are wrong. Recommended fix: derive the timestamp from the real arrival clock (arrivalUs*90000/1e6); minimal fix: step = 90000/fps.
nodes.norm_label: p1-b — rtp timestamp frozen at synthetic 60 fps

nodes.label: Homemade i18n runtime (frontend/js/i18n/i18n.js)
nodes.rationale: No build step or bundler (ES6 modules served statically) so the runtime is deliberately tiny (~90 lines, zero external dependency): init/t/applyDOM/setLanguage; dotted-key resolution with {{var}} interpolation; missing key → English fallback → raw key so the UI never breaks; language detection localStorage['mw-lang'] → navigator.language → en; 279 keys across 13 namespaces; parity enforced by scripts/check-i18n.cjs.

nodes.label: Tolgee self-hosted (translation management editor)
nodes.rationale: The runtime does NOT depend on Tolgee: Tolgee is a decoupled editor synced with frontend/locales/*.json via the Tolgee CLI push/pull using the JSON_I18NEXT format (nested keys + {{var}}), identical to the repo files — no conversion.

nodes.label: Tolgee Docker service (all-in-one, port 8085)
nodes.rationale: All-in-one image embeds PostgreSQL; data persists in a named volume; initial admin/admin user seeded (to change in production); for production point SPRING_DATASOURCE_* at an external Postgres.

nodes.label: Internet Access plan (PowerDNS + ACME + UPnP + WebRTC direct)
nodes.rationale: Full feature spec: GDPR consent screen (default OFF, public IP shared only with the self-hosted DNS service), settings schema, pending_registration retry when offline, periodic 5-min IP/DNS checks, security notes (token encrypted at rest, key perms 600). TURN explicitly excluded — direct connection only.
nodes.norm_label: internet access plan (powerdns + acme + upnp + webrtc direct)

nodes.label: Unique-ID subdomain registration via PowerDNS API
nodes.rationale: Reuses the existing uniqueid; checks rrset availability in the zone, retries with a new id up to 5 times on collision; A record created/updated via PATCH rrsets (TTL 300, changetype REPLACE); placeholder 127.0.0.1 when STUN fails at install.
nodes.norm_label: unique-id subdomain registration via powerdns api

nodes.label: ACME TLS certificate via DNS-01 challenge
nodes.rationale: DNS-01 against the self-hosted PowerDNS zone means no port 80 needed; provider ZeroSSL (per-instance EAB) or Let's Encrypt via lego; auto-renew when <30 days remain, hot TLS reload without restart; fallback to self-signed on issuance failure.
nodes.norm_label: acme tls certificate via dns-01 challenge

nodes.label: Transport auto-selection priority chain (media/DC × UDP/TCP → WSS)
nodes.rationale: Auto mode tries: WebRTC MediaTrack UDP → DataChannel UDP → MediaTrack TCP → DataChannel TCP → WSS; codec validated against RTCRtpReceiver.getCapabilities() before committing; ICE candidates sorted UDP host > UDP srflx > TCP; no TURN candidates ever (direct only).
nodes.norm_label: transport auto-selection priority chain (media/dc × udp/tcp → wss)

nodes.label: Limelight C API (LiStartConnection, callbacks, Limelight.h)
nodes.rationale: Public API of moonlight-common-c: LiStartConnection handles the whole RTSP handshake; drSubmitDecodeUnit/arDecodeAndPlaySample callbacks deliver video DECODE_UNITs and decoded PCM; LiSend*Event for input over the ENet control stream.

nodes.label: MoonlightWeb architecture & development plan (phases 1-8, [REEL] annotations)
nodes.rationale: Living plan updated to match real code: backend = HTTP/HTTPS server + Sunshine proxy + embedded moonlight-common-c; initial MVP transport was a single WebSocket multiplexing video/audio/input (channel-byte protocol), later superseded by WebRTC; estimated latency budget ~13-23 ms; documents which planned files never existed (VideoBridge/AudioBridge/InputBridge) and what replaced them.

nodes.label: MoonlightShim (C→Qt bridge for moonlight-common-c)
nodes.rationale: QObject wrapper running LiStartConnection on a dedicated QThread; converts Limelight C callbacks into Qt signals videoFrameReady(QByteArray, frameType, frameNumber) and audioSampleReady(QByteArray); thread handoff via Qt AutoConnection instead of a lock-free queue.

nodes.label: StreamRelay (WebSocket relay: video/audio/input multiplexed)
nodes.rationale: Single WebSocket multiplexes [channel:1][flags:1][payload:N] — video 0x01 (H.264 Annex B NALs, flag bit0=IDR), audio 0x02 (PCM16 LE); replaced the planned Video/Audio/InputBridge trio; head-of-line blocking judged negligible on LAN; now the WSS fallback transport.

nodes.label: EnetControlStream (ENet control channel: START_A/B + input)
nodes.rationale: Uses the ENet embedded in moonlight-common-c directly instead of the planned WebSocket bridge; serviced by a 20 ms QTimer on the main thread.

nodes.label: InputCrypto (AES-128-GCM input encryption)
nodes.rationale: OpenSSL EVP implementation matching moonlight-common-c's expectations, with IV rotation.

nodes.label: Mp4Muxer.js (minimal fMP4 muxer + NAL utilities)
nodes.rationale: Originally wrapped H.264 Annex B NALs into fMP4 segments for MSE; survives as the NAL toolbox (splitNals, VPS/SPS/PPS parser, hvcC/avcC description builder, toAvcc, codec strings) used by the WebCodecs path.

nodes.label: StreamView.js (frontend streaming view: decode + render + input + stats)
nodes.rationale: Absorbed all planned frontend streaming modules (StreamSession/VideoPipeline/AudioPipeline/input handlers); grew to ~4700 lines — flagged as standalone technical debt by the UI audit; hosts the WebCodecs VideoDecoder, Canvas 2D rendering with platform HEVC workarounds, and stats overlay.
nodes.norm_label: streamview.js (frontend streaming view: decode + render + input + stats)

nodes.label: Video passthrough decision (no decode/re-encode in backend)
nodes.rationale: NAL units assembled by moonlight-common-c are forwarded to the browser untouched (Annex B, SPS/PPS in-band); backend decode + re-encode would add 30ms+ and was never considered; the browser does the GPU decode.
nodes.norm_label: video passthrough decision (no decode/re-encode in backend)

nodes.label: MSE + fMP4 over WebCodecs (initial MVP decision, later reversed)
nodes.rationale: MSE was chosen for the MVP because it avoided manual timestamp management and A/V sync code; the shipped pipeline later moved to WebCodecs VideoDecoder + canvas (as documented in the HEVC audit prompt), inverting this decision.
nodes.norm_label: mse + fmp4 over webcodecs (initial mvp decision, later reversed)

nodes.label: Sync/async REST routes with ResponseCallback
nodes.rationale: Routes doing HTTPS to Sunshine (applist, launch, quit, boxart) are async with a deferred ResponseCallback (30 s timeout → 504) to avoid nested QEventLoops that caused 'Operation canceled' with concurrent TLS sessions; HTTPS requests are additionally serialized one-at-a-time per host.

nodes.label: Sunshine API reference (GameStream NvHTTP + REST)
nodes.rationale: Distinguishes the two APIs: GameStream XML API (HTTP 47989 / HTTPS 47990, mutual TLS) used for streaming, and the modern REST API (same HTTPS port, Basic Auth) not used by MoonlightWeb. Key implementation notes: uniqueid=0123456789ABCDEF is a fixed value shared by ALL Moonlight clients (the real client identity is the X.509 cert); HTTPS port is dynamic via <HttpsPort>; one HTTPS request at a time per host.

nodes.label: GameStream protocol (NvHTTP XML + RTSP + UDP flows)
nodes.rationale: Three layers: HTTP/HTTPS for discovery/pairing/launch, RTSP for session negotiation, UDP for video (47998) / audio (47999) / control (48000); ServerCodecModeSupport bitmask advertises H.264/HEVC/AV1 including 4:4:4 and 10-bit profiles.
nodes.norm_label: gamestream protocol (nvhttp xml + rtsp + udp flows)

nodes.label: Testing & non-regression guide (two-layer TNR gate)
nodes.rationale: Frontend: Vitest (jsdom) + @vitest/coverage-v8; backend: Qt assertion runner + OpenCppCoverage; scripts/run-tests.sh aggregates both. Tests assert observable behavior (inputs → outputs, emitted bytes, persisted JSON), never private internals, so refactors stay free.

nodes.label: 70% coverage gate scoped to pure-logic units only
nodes.rationale: The gate is measured only over deterministic pure-logic modules; the heavy I/O glue (WebRTC relays, sockets, Qt event loop, DOM, WebGPU/WebCodecs, worklets) is deliberately out of scope because covering it would require large brittle mocks that couple tests to the implementation — the opposite of a TNR safety net.
nodes.norm_label: 70% coverage gate scoped to pure-logic units only

nodes.label: frontend/index.html (app shell: header + main + footer, PWA meta)
nodes.rationale: PWA standalone meta so home-screen launch gives true fullscreen on iOS (browser chrome blocks the Fullscreen API on canvas); loads the modular design-system CSS in order tokens → base → layout → components → views → stream with ?v= cache-busting; static strings translated via data-i18n / data-i18n-attr attributes; single module entry js/app.js.
nodes.norm_label: frontend/index.html (app shell: header + main + footer, pwa meta)

nodes.label: Frontend mission prompt — stats overlay rework (StreamView)
nodes.rationale: Task spec: remove the codec badge and status dot from the stream header (setStatus becomes a no-op to avoid breaking its 7+ call sites), move the stats overlay to a top-center glass card (left-aligned on mobile to avoid the Stop button), switch _updateOverlay to rich innerHTML, keep all stat computations (sliding FPS/RTT/decode latency) unchanged.
nodes.norm_label: frontend mission prompt — stats overlay rework (streamview)
